$comment: Hand-authored content layered over the mechanical ZIL extraction. Merged by app/scripts/extract-zork.ts after the ZIL pass. Per-id deep merge for passages/rooms/items (sides matched by roomId, exits shallow-merged); customTools and triggers are appended.
$startStateComment: Initial flag values. player-health drives the player's HP for combat (drained by combat triggers; player-killed trigger ends the game when it hits zero). Boolean flags are pre-initialized to false so that `flag(key, false)` Conditions evaluate correctly — without this, an unset flag is `undefined` and `undefined === false` is false (strict equality in evaluateCondition), so 'not yet flipped' gates would silently never activate.

startState.$puzzleFlagsComment: Pre-init for the Phase-4 puzzle batch. Each is paired to its setter trigger below; pre-init avoids the unset-vs-false strict-equality gotcha when triggers gate on flag(X, false).

$darknessComment: Darkness model: a room is dark when it declares state.dark === true and no perceivable item has state.isLit === true. Composed from generic primitives (currentRoomState + anyPerceivableItemWith) — engine has no hardcoded darkness concept.

sharedVariants.text: It is pitch black. You are likely to be eaten by a grue.

$templatesComment: Build-time partial-Item templates. Items declare `fromTemplate: 'name'` to inherit fields; the extractor merges (item fields win at the leaf, arrays union, nested objects deep-merged) and strips `fromTemplate` from the final story. Engine never sees templates. Define-once, instantiate-many.

templates.$bladed-weapon-comment: Shared shape for sword, axe, knife. State keys (damage, weapon-stuck, attack-bonus-add/pct) are conventions class triggers gate on. Tags compose downstream.

passages.$comment: FRONT-DOOR. Connects west-of-house (outside) to living-room (inside). Solid oak banded with iron, barred from the inside — there's no handle on the inner face. Permanently sealed; the player must use the kitchen-window to enter the house. traversableWhen always false; per-side names so the inside view reads 'wooden door' and the outside reads 'front door'.
passages.name: front door
passages.description: Solid oak crossed with thick iron bands. There is no handle on this side and no obvious way to open it.
passages.narratorNote: This door is sealed from within — the player cannot open it from either side. It is the canonical 'barred front door'. When the player tries to open it or go through it, narrate the door's solid construction (heavy oak, iron bands, no handle, no keyhole on this side). Direct them to find another way in (the kitchen window) or out (likewise).

sides.name: front door
sides.description: The handsome front door of the white house — solid oak, iron-banded, evidently barred from within.

sides.name: wooden door
sides.description: A heavy oak door crossed with thick iron bands — the front door, barred shut from this side. No handle, no keyhole.

passages.traverseBlockedMessage: The door is barred shut from the inside — solid oak, iron-banded, no handle on this side. You'll need to find another way through.

passages.description: A small kitchen window. It appears to be slightly ajar.

variants.text: The kitchen window is open wide enough to climb through.

glimpse.prompt: Through the window, describe the area behind the house briefly — open ground sloping toward forest. Keep it to a sentence.
Through the window, describe the kitchen briefly — emphasize warmth and signs of recent cooking. Keep it to a sentence.

passages.$comment: Chimney is a CLIMBBIT/LOCAL-GLOBAL scenery in ZIL, not a DOORBIT, so mechanical extraction won't catch it. Hand-authored. Canonical Zork rules: kitchen→studio (down) is never traversable; studio→kitchen (up) requires the lamp and ≤2 carried items.
passages.name: chimney
passages.description: A dark, narrow chimney rising from the studio fireplace up to the kitchen above.

sides.traverseBlockedMessage: Only Santa Claus climbs down chimneys.
You can't get up there with what you're carrying.

passages.$comment: Trap door is HIDDEN under the rug in the living room. visibleWhen filters it out of passagesHere (so view + examine both refuse to surface it) until rug-moved-flag flips. openWhen gates the open intent: rug must be moved AND player must NOT be in the cellar (no handle on the underside — once it slams shut you can't reopen it from below; you have to loop back through the underground to the kitchen). traversableWhen gates traversal so a closed door can't be walked through.
passages.description: A wooden trap door is set into the floor.

passages.$comment: Grate is locked from above; opens only with the skeleton key (item id: keys, found in the maze near the skeleton). Symmetric — once unlocked, can open from either side. traversableWhen gates traversal on its open state.
passages.description: A heavy iron grating set into the floor — or, depending on which side you stand on, into the ceiling. Beyond it lies a clearing of scattered leaves.

rooms.$comment: Reservoir is the canonical missing room description — the ZIL routine walker didn't catch its M-LOOK branch. Authored prose here matches Infocom voice.
rooms.description: You are in a vast underground chamber, half-filled with cold dark water. The reservoir stretches north and south, fed from the north and drained by the dam to the south. Boats might cross when the water is high; at low tide, mud and silt stretch where the water once stood.

rooms.$comment: kitchen.down: ZIL has CEXIT against FALSE-FLAG (a never-true sentinel) — mechanical extraction produces an unreachable flag-gated exit. Replace with a passage exit so the chimney passage's traversableWhen drives it.
studio.up: ZIL has FEXIT (PER UP-CHIMNEY-FUNCTION) — skipped by mechanical extraction. Add it as a passage exit.

grating-clearing.down: ZIL extraction wired the up-direction (grating-room.up → grating-clearing) but left the reverse off the clearing side, so even with the skeleton key in hand the player can't descend back through the grate. Add the down exit referencing the grate passage; traversableWhen on the passage already gates on isOpen, so the closed-grating message still applies symmetrically.

living-room.down: ZIL declares the trap-door from cellar's side (cellar.up → living-room), but the descent direction from the living room itself is buried in routine logic the mechanical extractor skips. Add it explicitly so the player can actually descend through the open trap door. visibleWhen hides the exit until the rug is moved (so 'down' isn't even listed as a direction).

white-cliffs-north: ZIL gates south + west on flag(deflate) — the canonical 'you're on foot, not in the raft' check for the unwired magic-boat puzzle. Since deflate is never set, both exits are permanently blocked, soft-locking the player who walks east from damp-cave (which has NO matching gate, so entry is one-way). Strip the gates so the beach is navigable on foot. When the boat puzzle eventually lands, re-add the gating with the proper boat-state condition.
white-cliffs-south: same deflate-flag soft-lock as north. Ungate so the player can walk back north along the beach.
sandy-beach still has the dig-with-shovel scarab dev note — that puzzle remains unwired even after the boat ships.
rooms.description:
You are on a large sandy beach on the east shore of the river, which is flowing quickly by. A path runs beside the river to the south here, and a passage is partially buried in sand to the northeast.

[DEV NOTE: dig-with-shovel scarab puzzle is unwired. The scarab is currently visible without digging — canonical Zork required four DIG WITH SHOVEL actions to unbury it.]

This is a sand-filled cave whose exit is to the southwest.

[DEV NOTE: dig-with-shovel scarab puzzle is unwired. The scarab is currently visible here without digging — canonical Zork required four DIG WITH SHOVEL actions to unbury it.]

rooms.$comment: DARK ROOMS: canonical Zork I dark rooms get state.dark=true. Combined with the player having no perceivable lit light source, this satisfies the story-level darkness condition (defaultVisibility) and triggers the pitch-black sharedVariant + the grue tick triggers below.

items.$comment: Authored descriptions — strictly item-focused (no positional language). Voice: terse, concrete, faintly dry — Infocom house style. Describe what the THING looks/feels like, never where it sits.
items.description: Dense pine and hemlock, the canopy filtering daylight to a green twilight.
A large leaf-bearing tree, lower branches at climbing height.
An impassable wall of high peaks — the Flatheads, named (legend has it) for their first king.
Cold dark water moving with quiet, steady force. The current is swift; rocks lie half-hidden below.
Sheer chalk-white cliffs, scoured smooth and quite unscalable.
A rough cliff face, weathered into ledges and handholds. Climbing it is possible — and perilous.
A perfect arc of color, shimmering with a strange and almost solid presence.
Fine pale sand, soft underfoot — the sort that responds well to a shovel.
A narrow vertical crack — far too thin for anyone to slip through.
Cold dark water — drinkable, you suppose, though the thought isn't appetizing.
A measure of clear water, transparent and quite ordinary.
Translucent figures drifting through the air, faces blank with ancient grief. They take no notice of you.
A heap of corpses, far too long-dead to identify. Disturbing them feels like a very poor idea.
A sleek vampire bat, watching with bright unblinking eyes — and a mouth full of small sharp teeth.
An inscription in an ancient script — a prayer of safe passage, you somehow understand.
A window securely boarded over from within. There's no entering this way.
A great mound of weathered stone, ancient beyond reckoning, with a single massive door.
An enormous stone door, impossibly heavy. It would take more than human strength to budge.
A narrow flight of stone steps ascending into shadow.
A sturdy wooden ladder, rungs worn smooth by use.
A worn wooden railing, polished smooth by countless hands.
Flood Control Dam #3 — a vast concrete structure of evident bureaucratic origin, holding back an enormous body of water.
A bank of indicator lights, four colored buttons, and a single iron bolt — clearly some kind of maintenance station.
A heavy iron bolt with a slot evidently sized for a wrench.
A small green glass bubble — currently dark, evidently waiting for something.
A worn yellow button, slightly recessed. Yellow paint flakes around the edges.
A small brown button, smooth and unmarked.
A red button marked with a faded warning symbol.
A small blue button, smooth and unmarked.
Water trickles from a small leak — for now a nuisance rather than a disaster.
A complicated piece of machinery: gears, curious nozzles, a hinged lid, and a small switch on one flank.
A small switch with a slot evidently sized for a screwdriver.
A long metal chute angling steeply downward into darkness. Once you commit, climbing back out looks impossible.
A length of broken timber, splintered at one end and surprisingly heavy.
A small brass bell with a worn handle, polished smooth by use.
A weathered skeleton, long picked clean. A small leather bag lies near one outstretched hand.
A pungent clove of garlic, its papery skin crackling at the slightest touch.
A small hand-held pump — the sort you'd use for a bicycle tire, or perhaps a folded raft.
A lump of viscous greyish material, evidently good for sealing a puncture or two.
A standard flat-head screwdriver, well-worn but serviceable.
A long-handled shovel, well-balanced for digging.
A solid iron wrench, sized for a familiar bolt.
A small heap of dusty black coal — surprisingly heavy, and very dirty.
A flawless emerald the size of a small egg, its green depths catching every gleam of light. A treasure.
An exquisite scarab beetle carved from a single piece of jade and chased with gold. A treasure.
A delicate bracelet set with sapphires, glittering even in dim light. A treasure.
A large wicker basket suspended from a chain, currently lowered.
A large wicker basket suspended from a chain, currently raised.
A large black book bound in cracked leather, held open to page 569.
An elongated brown paper sack, smelling powerfully of hot peppers.
A silver chalice, intricately engraved with looping vines. A treasure.
Poseidon's own trident, carved from a single piece of crystal. A treasure.
The solid-gold coffin used for the burial of Ramses II — heavy, ornate, and worth a king's ransom. A treasure.
An exquisite jade figurine, carved with delicate features. A treasure.
A battered brass lantern, its battery long since drained dry.
A matchbook whose cover advertises FCD#3 in chipper bureaucratic prose.
A pair of long burning candles, dripping wax steadily.
A pile of dry leaves, gathered as if by wind.
A red metal buoy, weathered by long exposure — evidently a warning.
An old wooden trunk, mud-caked and bulging with jewels. A treasure.
A small metal tube of the sort that holds toothpaste — but its contents are something else entirely.
Old engravings worked deep into stone — ancient writing, half worn away.
A small bird's nest, woven of twigs and grass.
A golden clockwork canary with ruby eyes and a silver beak. A crystal window below its left wing reveals intricate machinery; the mechanism appears to have wound down. A treasure.
An enormous wall-sized mirror, polished to a perfect reflection.
An enormous wall-sized mirror, polished to a perfect reflection.

items.$comment: SURFACE: kitchen-table is canonically a flat surface (CONTBIT in ZIL so the parser handles 'put X on table', no OPENBIT). state.isOpen + accessibleWhen are nulled out so the close handler refuses ('isn't something you can close'). Real description + narratorNote tell the LLM how to handle the on-vs-in mismatch.
items.description: A long wooden kitchen table, scarred by years of food preparation.
items.narratorNote: This is a flat work surface, not a closeable container. Items located 'in' the kitchen-table (sandwich-bag, bottle) are actually resting ON its surface — narrate them as such. Never refer to the table as 'open' or 'closed'.

items.$comment: SURFACE: attic-table — same shape as kitchen-table.
items.description: A heavy old wooden table, varnish worn through in patches.
items.narratorNote: Flat surface, not a closeable container. Items located 'in' this table are resting ON its surface. Never refer to the table as 'open' or 'closed'.

items.$comment: SURFACE: altar in south-temple. A flat stone surface for offerings.
items.description: A massive stone altar, carved with worn ritual figures and stained dark by centuries of offerings.
items.narratorNote: The altar is a flat ritual surface, not a closeable container. Items located 'in' the altar (candles, the black book) are actually resting ON top of it. Never refer to the altar as 'open' or 'closed'.

items.$comment: SURFACE: pedestal in torch-room. A stone column with a torch on top.
items.description: A polished stone pedestal, waist-high.
items.narratorNote: A short column, not a container. Items located 'in' the pedestal (the torch) are resting ON its top. Never refer to the pedestal as 'open' or 'closed'.

items.$comment: Egg puzzle: the clasp is too delicate for direct opening. Suppress the auto-gen 'open egg' intent (openWhen: never) and let the give-egg-to-songbird intent + trigger handle opening. Force-opening (smashing) destroys the canary inside — that's a future trigger.

Brass lantern with battery. state.isLit governs whether it emits light (per the perceivable-light convention used by defaultVisibility). state.batteryTurns counts down each turn while lit; at 0 the lamp auto-extinguishes (lamp-runs-out tick trigger). Canonical Zork battery is ~330 turns.

Troll for combat. fromTemplate gives him the shared troll personality + status state (unconscious/knocked-down/disarmed/hostile baseline) plus the enemy/troll/humanoid tags. Per-instance fields below: state.health (10) and the description.
items.personality: A nasty, hulking creature with greasy black hair and broken yellow tusks. Speaks in broken Common — short sentences, aggressive, third person ('Troll smash!'). Hates elves and dwarves; covets shiny things. If addressed in a friendly tone, suspicious. If threatened, roars. If knocked unconscious, snores and mutters about gold. Will not converse meaningfully — short menacing lines only.

items.$comment: Elvish sword. fromTemplate('bladed-weapon') gives it the weapon/bladed/carryable tags + base weapon state (weapon-stuck, attack-bonus-*). Tags 'sword' and 'magical' are unioned in. damage:5 layered on per-instance. variants pulse the description to 'glowing' when a hostile is perceivable.
items.description: An elvish sword of great antiquity, the blade etched with fine runes.

variants.text: An elvish sword of great antiquity, the blade etched with fine runes — and presently glowing with a faint blue light.

items.$comment: Rusty knife — feeble bladed weapon. fromTemplate provides shared bladed-weapon shape; tag 'knife' unioned in; damage 2.
items.description: A rusty knife, the blade pitted but still serviceable.

items.$comment: Troll's axe. After the troll drops it (disarm outcome OR death), the player can pick it up and either swing it or throw it — different mode triggers, different outcome tables. fromTemplate gives shared bladed-weapon state; tags 'axe' and 'throwable' unioned in.
items.description: A heavy iron axe, its head crusted with dried blood.

items.$comment: leaflet item id is 'advertisement' in the ZIL extraction (the mailbox leaflet that explains the game).
items.description: A small printed leaflet, the paper a little crumpled.

items.$comment: Nasty knife — the thief's weapon. Tag it as bladed so the class trigger covers it too if the player ever wields it.
items.description: A nasty-looking knife, the blade dull but evidently well-used.
A hot pepper sandwich wrapped tightly in waxed paper. Smells fierce.
A clear glass bottle, sealed at the neck.
A small mailbox of dented metal, fixed firmly to a post outside the front door.
A handsome glass-fronted display case — clearly meant to hold a collector's prizes.

items.personality: A bright forest songbird with a clear, perfect trill. Doesn't speak — communicates through song. Curious about shiny things, especially the jewel-encrusted egg. Gentle, never threatening.
items.description: A small bright-feathered songbird, watching you with curious black eyes. Its trill is impossibly pure.

items.$comment: Cyclops gets HP, hunger counter, and combat status state. hunger ticks up while the player lingers in his room; at threshold he attacks. Health is high (huge enemy); class triggers will favor misses over kills.
items.personality: A massive, single-eyed brute, hungry and angry. Speaks in slow rumbling Common — full sentences but simple words. Demands food and drink. Terrified of the name of the Greek hero who blinded his ancestor. If fed, becomes drowsy and friendly; if threatened, roars and lunges.

items.$comment: Thief gets HP, autonomy state. appeared:false until the global turn counter reaches the threshold (30 turns). openWhen+closeWhen suppress the auto-gen open/close intents that fire because the extractor saw the thief as a CONTBIT (he carries stolen things). Players opening the thief is silly; the player attacking him for his loot is the path.
items.personality: A suave, mustachioed gentleman thief in a black cloak. Speaks in clipped, ironic phrases ('How droll.', 'A trifle, surely?'). Will steal valuables on sight; pockets things and vanishes. Treats every encounter as an aesthetic event — never crude, always theatrical.
items.description: A suspicious-looking gentleman in a black cloak, holding a large bag and armed with a deadly stiletto.
A long coil of strong rope.
A heavy oriental carpet covers the center of the floor, oddly out of place. Something about its position invites curiosity.
A skeleton key of dark iron, deeply worn — clearly very old.
A flaming torch, the haft carved from ivory and the head wreathed in clean flame. A treasure.

items.$comment: Maze treasure. Listed in win condition; tagging it 'treasure' lets future score-counter triggers walk all treasure-tagged items rather than enumerate by id.
items.description: An old leather bag, bulging with coins. A treasure.

items.$comment: Land of the Living Dead treasure. The crystal skull of an ancient adventurer.
items.description: A beautifully carved crystal skull, grinning at you rather nastily. A treasure.

items.$comment: Echo-room treasure. Hidden by SACREDBIT in canonical Zork until ECHO is spoken in loud-room. We adapt via visibleWhen on echo-spoken — bar isn't perceivable until the echo trigger fires.
items.description: A heavy platinum bar, dense and gleaming dully. A treasure.

items.$comment: Rainbow treasure. INVISIBLE in canonical Zork until the player waves the sceptre at end-of-rainbow. We adapt via visibleWhen on rainbow-flag.
items.description: A heavy iron pot brimming with gold coins. A treasure.

items.$comment: NEW item appended via mergeItems — diamond produced by the coal-machine puzzle. Starts at 'nowhere'; the machine-trigger moves it to the machine on success.
items.name: huge diamond
items.description: A magnificent flawless diamond, gleaming with inner fire.

items.$comment: Tag scepter as a weapon so the inventoryHasTag(weapon) check on the boat catches it. Canonical Zork includes SCEPTRE in the boat-puncture list because it has WEAPONBIT — the magic boat treats it as sharp.
Tag thief's stiletto as a weapon (canonical Zork has it in WEAPONBIT). Used for the boat-puncture check.
items.description: A slim, wickedly pointed stiletto — the thief's signature weapon.

items.$comment: Make the inflatable boat a real engine vehicle. state.inflation: 'deflated' (default) | 'inflated' | 'punctured'. The vehicle.enterableWhen gate ensures players can only board the inflated form. mobile=true so the boat travels with the player on go() — drifts downstream with the current.

vehicle.enterBlockedMessage: The boat is a flat pile of plastic — it'd need to be inflated first.

items.description: A folded square of yellow plastic with a small valve attached — clearly intended to be inflated.

$customToolsComment: Custom tools are author-defined LLM tools. Each becomes a tool the narrator can call directly with typed args. Handlers do the generic state mutation (open sets isOpen=true on any item with that state key); per-puzzle special cases are layered on as triggers that match on intentMatched + intentArg. open/close/break here replace the dozens of per-item intents that the auto-gen used to emit.

customTools.$comment: SCORE verb. Player asks for current standing; engine emits the canonical Zork phrasing with live values via narration template tokens.
customTools.description: Player asks about their current score, rank, moves, or progress (e.g. 'score', 'how am I doing?', 'what's my score?'). Always available.

handler.successNarration: Your score is {flag.score} (total of {flag.max-score}), in {flag.global-turn-count} moves. This gives you the rank of {rank}.

customTools.$comment: Open handler — works for both items (mailbox, trophy case) AND passages (kitchen window, trap door). The arg is named itemId for backward compat, but the value can be either an item id or a passage id from the view. Each precondition is an OR over item+passage variants; effects emit BOTH setItemState and setPassageState — the one matching the id mutates, the other defensively no-ops.
customTools.description: Player opens, pulls open, lifts the lid of, unlatches, or otherwise opens an item or passage (door, window, lid, etc.). Pass the id from the current view's itemsHere or passagesHere. The engine's handler reports failure when the target isn't accessible, isn't openable, or is already open — narrate from those cues.

itemId.description: the id of the item or passage to open (from itemsHere or passagesHere in the current view)

customTools.$comment: Close handler — symmetric to open. Same item-or-passage polymorphism.
customTools.description: Player closes, shuts, latches, lids, or otherwise closes an item or passage (door, window, lid, etc.).

itemId.description: the id of the item or passage to close (from itemsHere or passagesHere in the current view)

customTools.description: Player breaks, smashes, shatters, hurls, or otherwise destroys an item. Once broken, an item stays broken — opening or repairing it requires a separate puzzle.

preconditions.failedNarration: {arg.itemId.name} doesn't seem like something you could break.

customTools.$comment: Rope-and-dome puzzle. Player ties the rope to the railing in the Dome Room; that flips dome-flag, unlocking the existing dome-room.down → torch-room exit. Active only when you're standing in the dome with the rope (and haven't already done it — once-matched intents auto-deactivate).
customTools.description: Player ties, fastens, attaches, lashes, secures, or otherwise affixes the rope to the railing (or to the dome).

customTools.$comment: Egg puzzle: give the jewel-encrusted egg to the songbird; her song springs the clasp open and reveals the canary. Songbird is in forest-1 with appearsIn covering forest-2/3, path, up-a-tree, clearing — gate on player being in any of those. Inactive after egg is open.
customTools.description: Player gives the jewel-encrusted egg to the songbird, offers it to the bird, hands it to her, presents it to the singer.

customTools.$comment: Move-the-rug puzzle. Living-room rug hides the trap door. Once moved, rug-moved-flag flips and the trap-door's openWhen passes. Active only when in living-room and not yet matched.
customTools.description: Player moves, slides, lifts, pulls aside, kicks aside, rolls up, or otherwise displaces the rug (carpet) in the living room.

customTools.$comment: Cyclops magic word. Saying 'ulysses' or 'odysseus' (referencing the Greek hero who blinded the cyclops Polyphemus) terrifies him. He flees east, smashing through the wall to make a new passage to the living room. Sets both cyclops-flag (he's gone, can climb up to treasure room) and magic-flag (the wall is broken, opens cyclops-room↔living-room shortcut).
customTools.description: Player says, shouts, whispers, or invokes 'Ulysses', 'Odysseus', or otherwise utters the magic word that terrifies the cyclops.

customTools.$comment: Cyclops feed alternative. Give him the hot pepper sandwich (lunch) and water bottle. He eats, drinks, and falls asleep. Sets cyclops-flag (no longer blocking) but NOT magic-flag (no wall smash — slept, didn't flee). Different aftermath than the magic word.
customTools.description: Player gives, offers, or hands the cyclops the lunch (hot pepper sandwich) and the bottle of water — feeding and watering him.

customTools.$comment: Pray at the altar in south-temple. Teleports the player to forest-1 (canonical Zork: prayer at the altar transports the adventurer back to the surface).
customTools.description: Player prays, kneels, makes an offering, or performs a ritual at the altar.

customTools.$comment: Light the brass lantern. Active when carrying it, currently unlit, and battery has charge remaining.
customTools.description: Player turns on, lights, switches on, fires up, or activates the brass lantern.

customTools.$comment: Extinguish the brass lantern. Active whenever it's lit (regardless of who's holding it).
customTools.description: Player turns off, douses, switches off, or extinguishes the brass lantern.

customTools.$comment: Player tries to free a stuck weapon (sword/axe/knife). Active whenever any of the bladed weapons is in the weapon-stuck state. The trigger picks the right weapon by checking each individually.
customTools.description: Player tries to free, yank, pull, dislodge, retrieve, or wrench loose their stuck weapon (sword, knife, or axe).

customTools.$comment: TALK-TO-TROLL: player engages the troll conversationally. Each match bumps troll.state.aggravation; at threshold the troll snaps and starts attacking (NPC-initiated combat). Active only while the troll is alive, present, and not yet engaged. The narrator records this BEFORE narrating the troll's response in character.
customTools.description: Player tries to talk to, address, ask, shout at, insult, threaten, bargain with, beg, taunt, or otherwise engage the troll conversationally.

customTools.$comment: TALK-TO-CYCLOPS: same shape as talk-to-troll. Bumps cyclops hunger by an extra +1 per talk (over the passive hunger tick), so an aggressive talker provokes him faster. Active while cyclops alive, present, not yet appeased.
customTools.description: Player tries to talk to, address, ask, taunt, insult, threaten, or otherwise engage the cyclops conversationally — but NOT saying 'Ulysses' or 'Odysseus' (those match cyclops-magic-word instead).

customTools.$comment: ECHO room — say 'echo' in loud-room. Bare verb in canonical Zork (special command loop in LOUD-ROOM-FCN). We accept any phrasing that's clearly invoking the echo: 'echo', 'say echo', 'shout echo', 'call out echo'.
customTools.description: Player says, shouts, calls, invokes, or otherwise utters the word 'echo' in the loud room.

customTools.$comment: WAVE SCEPTRE at the rainbow. Active at end-of-rainbow OR aragain-falls (per canonical SCEPTRE-FUNCTION). Requires the sceptre in inventory. Wave / raise / brandish / point all count.
customTools.description: Player waves, raises, brandishes, points, or otherwise invokes the sceptre at the rainbow.

customTools.$comment: RUB the mirror in either mirror-room. Canonical mechanic teleports the player to the OTHER mirror room. We accept rub / touch / press / lean against / step into.
customTools.description: Player rubs, touches, presses, leans against, steps into, or otherwise interacts physically with the mirror.

customTools.$comment: RING the bell. Canonical: bell must be in inventory; bell becomes hot-bell and drops to floor. Adapted: just track that it was rung. Active when player has bell.
customTools.description: Player rings, tolls, peals, sounds, or otherwise activates the bell.

customTools.$comment: READ the book — canonical capstone of the bell+candles+book ritual. Active at entrance-to-hades with book + bell already rung + candles still lit.
customTools.description: Player reads, recites, intones, or speaks aloud from the black book at the entrance to Hades.

customTools.$comment: TURN the machine switch (with screwdriver, when machine is closed and contains coal). Active in machine-room with the screwdriver, machine closed.
customTools.description: Player turns, presses, activates, or otherwise operates the machine's switch (with the screwdriver as a tool).

customTools.$comment: PUSH the YELLOW button — enables the bolt control. Active in maintenance-room. Bare press is canonical.
customTools.description: Player pushes, presses, depresses, or activates the yellow button.

customTools.$comment: PUSH the BROWN button — disables the bolt control. Active in maintenance-room.
customTools.description: Player pushes, presses, depresses, or activates the brown button.

customTools.$comment: TURN the dam bolt with wrench — toggles the gates. Requires gate-flag (yellow pressed) + wrench in inventory.
customTools.description: Player turns, twists, rotates, or otherwise operates the bolt on the dam control panel using the wrench.

customTools.$comment: BOAT INFLATE: pump the deflated raft. Requires hasItem(pump) and the boat in the same room (or carryable in inventory while deflated). Active only while deflated. Boarding/disembarking are engine actions (not intents) — the LLM calls board(itemId) directly.
customTools.description: Player inflates, pumps up, fills, or blows up the boat (with the pump).

customTools.$comment: BOAT REPAIR with putty (in tube). Active only when boat is punctured and player carries putty. Consumed on use.
customTools.description: Player plugs, repairs, patches, or applies putty to the punctured boat.

triggers.$comment: PUZZLE/VISIT BONUSES — round the 270-point treasure baseline to 350. Sum of these = 80.
triggers.narration: (Solving the dam puzzle. +15 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(Completing the bell-book-candle ritual at Hades. +5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(Ringing the bell at this threshold. +10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The troll defeated. +10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The cyclops dealt with. +10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The rainbow solidifies — passable now. +5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(A new passage opens. +5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You found the egg. +5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(Discovering the living room. +5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(Reaching the troll's lair. +5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(Discovering the cyclops's chamber. +5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the crystal skull's worth — +10 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the sceptre's worth — +4 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the chalice's worth — +10 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the crystal trident's worth — +4 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the gold coffin's worth — +10 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the huge diamond's worth — +10 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the jade figurine's worth — +5 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the leather bag of coins's worth — +10 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the large emerald's worth — +5 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the painting's worth — +4 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the platinum bar's worth — +10 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the pot of gold's worth — +10 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the sapphire-encrusted bracelet's worth — +5 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the beautiful jeweled scarab's worth — +5 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the torch's worth — +14 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the trunk of jewels's worth — +15 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the jewel-encrusted egg's worth — +5 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
(You note the golden clockwork canary's worth — +6 points. Your score is now {flag.score}.)
The crystal skull joins the trophy case's collection. (+10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The crystal skull leaves the trophy case. -10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The sceptre joins the trophy case's collection. (+6 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The sceptre leaves the trophy case. -6 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The chalice joins the trophy case's collection. (+5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The chalice leaves the trophy case. -5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The crystal trident joins the trophy case's collection. (+11 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The crystal trident leaves the trophy case. -11 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The gold coffin joins the trophy case's collection. (+15 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The gold coffin leaves the trophy case. -15 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The huge diamond joins the trophy case's collection. (+10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The huge diamond leaves the trophy case. -10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The jade figurine joins the trophy case's collection. (+5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The jade figurine leaves the trophy case. -5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The leather bag of coins joins the trophy case's collection. (+5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The leather bag of coins leaves the trophy case. -5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The large emerald joins the trophy case's collection. (+10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The large emerald leaves the trophy case. -10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The painting joins the trophy case's collection. (+6 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The painting leaves the trophy case. -6 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The platinum bar joins the trophy case's collection. (+5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The platinum bar leaves the trophy case. -5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The pot of gold joins the trophy case's collection. (+10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The pot of gold leaves the trophy case. -10 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The sapphire-encrusted bracelet joins the trophy case's collection. (+5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The sapphire-encrusted bracelet leaves the trophy case. -5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The beautiful jeweled scarab joins the trophy case's collection. (+5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The beautiful jeweled scarab leaves the trophy case. -5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The torch joins the trophy case's collection. (+6 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The torch leaves the trophy case. -6 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The trunk of jewels joins the trophy case's collection. (+5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The trunk of jewels leaves the trophy case. -5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The jewel-encrusted egg joins the trophy case's collection. (+5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The jewel-encrusted egg leaves the trophy case. -5 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
The golden clockwork canary joins the trophy case's collection. (+4 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
(The golden clockwork canary leaves the trophy case. -4 points; your score is now {flag.score}.)
You tie the rope securely to the railing. It hangs down into the room below.
You hold out the egg. The songbird hops to it and sings a single perfect trill — and the delicate clasp springs open, revealing a golden clockwork canary nestled inside.
You drag the rug aside. Beneath it, set flush into the floor, is a closed wooden trap door.

The cyclops's eye widens in stark terror. He bellows, scrambles backward, and crashes east through the wall — leaving a cyclops-shaped hole that opens straight into the living room. The way up the staircase is now unguarded.
The cyclops devours the sandwich in two bites and drains the bottle. Within moments his single eye droops, and he slumps against the wall, snoring thunderously. The staircase is no longer guarded.

triggers.$comment: Consume the intent so the player can pray again later from the temple. Without removeMatchedIntent the trigger would re-fire every turn after the first prayer (matchedIntents persists), teleporting the player on every action.
triggers.narration: You kneel before the altar. A gentle warmth gathers around you, and the temple fades. When your senses clear, you are standing in a sunlit forest.

triggers.$comment: TROLL COMBAT — STATUS-AWARE OVERRIDE. Player attacks an unconscious troll for an instant kill, regardless of which weapon. Listed first via priority 100 so it consumes attack-this-turn before any class trigger fires. Demonstrates the pattern: more-specific triggers (status-aware) win over general (class).
triggers.narration: The troll lies helpless. Your weapon descends and ends him with a single, brutal blow.

triggers.$comment: MODE OVERRIDE: throwing the axe at the troll. Listed at priority 50 (above the class trigger) and gated on attack-mode='throw'. Either hits or misses, but the axe always ends up on the floor (player loses the weapon either way — that's what throwing means).

branches.narration: The axe whirls end-over-end and buries itself in the troll's chest. He topples.
The axe slams into the troll's shoulder, drawing blood. He pulls it free and drops it.
The axe sails wide and clatters against the wall.

triggers.$comment: SPECIFIC OVERRIDE: rusty knife is feeble vs the troll. Lower kill weight, less damage, more misses than the class trigger. Priority 50 so it wins over the bladed-class trigger (priority 10).

branches.narration: Your rusty knife scratches the troll.
You stab the troll deep — the knife's tip snaps off.
The troll sneers and bats your knife aside.

triggers.$comment: CLASS TRIGGER: any bladed weapon vs the troll. Covers sword + axe (swing) + any future bladed weapon — one trigger via flagItemHasTag. Priority 10 (below specific overrides). Self-cleans attack-this-turn so the cleanup catchall is just safety. Refuses if weapon is stuck or troll is dead.

branches.narration: Your blade bites the troll for a light wound; greenish blood wells.
Your blade carves a serious gash across the troll's chest.
A killing blow! The troll roars and topples in a pool of greenish blood.
Your blade strikes the troll's temple — he crumples, unconscious.
Your blade sweeps the troll's leg — he goes down hard.
Your blade cracks the troll's axe-grip — the bloody axe spins to the floor.
Your weapon sticks fast in the troll's hide — you can't pull it free!
The troll dodges your swing.

triggers.$comment: TROLL'S SWING AT PLAYER. Tick trigger: fires every turn while combat-engaged-with-troll is set AND the troll can act (alive, not unconscious, not knocked-down). Drains flag('player-health'); player-killed trigger ends the game on zero.

branches.narration: The troll's axe nicks your arm.
The troll's axe bites deep!
The troll's axe strikes a mortal blow!
The troll swings wildly and misses.

triggers.$comment: Troll status recovery: knocked-down troll heaves himself back up next turn. Author chose immediate recovery; an alternative would be a multi-turn counter.
triggers.narration: The troll heaves himself back to his feet.

triggers.$comment: Combat disengages when the player flees the troll-room. Engagement flag clears so combat-troll-attacks-player stops ticking. The troll stays in the room (just no longer attacking the absent player).

Catchall: ensures attack-this-turn never persists across turns even when no specific/class trigger matched (e.g. attack troll with leaflet). Priority -100 so it fires last. Combat triggers self-clean redundantly; this is the safety net.

Stuck-weapon recovery (active): player explicitly tries to free their weapon. 60% success per attempt; takes a turn either way. Per-weapon branches because the engine has no way to ask 'which of these is currently stuck?' as a single Condition — three random weights, one per possible stuck weapon.

branches.narration: You wrench the weapon free.
You pull, but the weapon stays fast.

triggers.$comment: Stuck-weapon recovery (passive safety net): each turn while a weapon is stuck, count up; after 3 turns it works loose on its own. Ensures the player isn't permanently disarmed. One trigger per weapon — could be tag-driven later.

triggers.narration: Your sword finally works free with a wet jerk.
Your axe finally works free with a wet jerk.

triggers.$comment: Troll dies when his HP drops. Drops his axe (canonical), opens the east/west exits via troll-flag, removes the body. Clears the engagement flag so combat-troll-attacks-player stops. Regular trigger (cascades from combat tick effects).
triggers.narration: The troll collapses in a pool of greenish blood. His bloody axe falls at his feet. The east and west passages out of the room are no longer guarded.

triggers.$comment: Death-narration override: dying in maze-5 (the skeleton room) gets the canonical 'you have joined its inhabitants' flavor. Priority 100 + once:true means it fires before the generic player-killed trigger; it sets death-handled=true to consume the death event so player-killed's gate fails. Same consume-the-flag pattern used by the combat triggers' attack-this-turn cleanup.

effects.message:
**** You have died ****

Your breath rattles, and you slump beside the pile of bones. You have joined its inhabitants.

triggers.$comment: Generic player-death gate. Any trigger that drains player-health below zero ends the game with this catchall message — UNLESS a more specific death trigger (priority > 0, gated on context) consumes the event by setting flag('death-handled')=true. The message is intentionally context-free so it works for cyclops, troll, grue (oh no — actually grue has its own endGame), thief, or any future hostile.

effects.message:
**** You have died ****

Your wounds overcome you, and the world fades to black.

triggers.$comment: Canonical Zork: the first time the player descends into the cellar with the trap door open, an unseen hand (the thief, or some unknown agency) slams it shut from above. once:true so it's a unique one-time event. The player can't reopen it from below (trap-door.openWhen blocks playerAt(cellar)), so they have to loop through the underground (cellar → south → east-of-chasm → gallery → studio → up the chimney → kitchen → west → living-room) to reach the trap door from above again.
triggers.narration: From above, an unseen hand slams the trap door shut. You hear the click of a latch.

triggers.$comment: Consume the matched intent so the trigger doesn't auto-re-fire every turn (matchedIntents persists forever; without removeMatchedIntent the regular fixed-point loop would re-fire this on every iteration up to MAX_TRIGGER_ITERATIONS, flooding narrationCues with duplicate 'flickers on' lines).
triggers.narration: The brass lantern flickers on, casting a warm glow.
The lantern goes dark.

triggers.$comment: Tick: while the lamp is lit, drain one battery turn per action. afterAction + once:false fires each turn.
Tick: at zero battery, force-extinguish the lamp. Cascade trigger handled by phase-3 fixed-point. once:true so the canonical narration only fires once.
triggers.narration: Your lamp dims, flickers, and goes dark.

triggers.$comment: GRUE: ticks the darkness-turns counter while the player is in pitch black. Player dies on the SECOND consecutive dark turn — first turn shows pitch-black warning, second turn the grue gets them. Counter resets when light is restored.

effects.message: Oh, no! You have walked into the slavering fangs of a lurking grue!

triggers.$comment: GLOBAL TURN COUNTER. Reusable infra: increments every action, no gating. Drives the thief's first-appearance threshold and any future time-based puzzles. afterAction:true so it ticks even on rejected actions.

TROLL AGGRAVATION (NPC-INITIATED COMBAT). Every time the LLM matches the talk-to-troll intent, this tick fires. Bumps troll.state.aggravation by 1, consumes the intent so it can fire again next turn, and emits a growl cue. Three matches push him over the edge (see troll-snaps).
triggers.narration: The troll bares his yellow tusks and growls.

triggers.$comment: TROLL SNAPS — threshold trigger. When aggravation hits 3 and he isn't already engaged, flip combat-engaged-with-troll. The existing combat-troll-attacks-player tick takes over from the next turn. Priority 50 wins over passive baseline triggers.
triggers.narration: Your prattling has worn through the last shred of the troll's patience. 'TROLL SMASH!' he bellows, and lunges at you.

triggers.$comment: CYCLOPS HUNGER TICK. Each turn the player lingers in cyclops-room, his hunger ticks up. Inactive once cyclops-flag is set (he's been appeased — magic word OR fed). Random branches at hunger=3 and hunger=6 add menacing flavor cues; the actual engagement happens via cyclops-snaps below.
TALK-TO-CYCLOPS BUMP. Talking to the cyclops aggravates his hunger faster (+1 per talk on top of the passive tick). Consume intent so it can fire next turn.
triggers.narration: The cyclops eyes you with growing impatience.

triggers.$comment: CYCLOPS HUNGER MILESTONE — early warning at 3. Once-only, narrates the rising tension. No state change beyond a cue.
triggers.narration: The cyclops sniffs the air greedily and takes a step toward you.

triggers.$comment: CYCLOPS SNAPS — threshold trigger. Hunger >= 6 boots the cyclops into autonomous combat. Same engagement-flag pattern as the troll. Priority 50 wins over passive baselines.
triggers.narration: The cyclops's patience snaps. 'I HUNGER!' he bellows, and his great hand swings down at you!

triggers.$comment: CYCLOPS ATTACKS PLAYER. Tick trigger — fires every turn while engaged + cyclops can act. Cyclops is huge: harder hits than the troll, plus the unique 'lift and throw' / 'grab and crush' outcomes. Drains player-health.

branches.narration: The cyclops cuffs you with the back of his hand — your vision swims.
The cyclops's fist pounds you into the wall!
The cyclops grabs you in a crushing grip — you feel ribs crack!
The cyclops's massive fingers find your throat. Stars bloom behind your eyes.
The cyclops swings — too slow. You dance aside.

triggers.$comment: CYCLOPS BLADED CLASS TRIGGER. Player-initiated combat against the cyclops. He's huge — sword/axe/knife mostly bounce off; rare deep cut, no instant kill, no disarm or stun. Authors voice: cyclops is a tank. Priority 10 (below specific overrides if any are added later).

branches.narration: Your blade glances off the cyclops's hide — a shallow scratch. He laughs.
Your blade finds a gap in the cyclops's hide and bites deep!
You swing — the cyclops doesn't even flinch. Your blade rings off his skin.

triggers.$comment: CYCLOPS DIES. Mirror of troll-dies. Clears engagement, removes the cyclops, sets cyclops-flag (which the magic-word/feed paths also set — they unblock the staircase). Doesn't smash the wall; only the magic-word path does that (it sets magic-flag too).
triggers.narration: The cyclops's massive bulk shudders, then crashes to the floor with a sound like a falling tree. He does not move again. The staircase is no longer guarded.

triggers.$comment: CYCLOPS DISENGAGE on flee. Mirror of combat-disengage-on-flee for the troll.

THIEF FIRST APPEARS. After 30 global turns, the thief slips into one of the major underground rooms (random pick). Once-only; appeared:true gates further wandering. He starts in round-room per ZIL extraction; this trigger relocates him into the pool of wanderable rooms so he isn't always at the same first-encounter spot.
triggers.narration: Somewhere in the darkness, you hear the soft scuff of a boot on stone. You are no longer alone in the underground.

triggers.$comment: THIEF WANDERS. Each turn after he's appeared, small chance he moves to a new wanderable room. Gated on player NOT being in his current room (so he doesn't slip away the moment the player arrives). Weights heavily favor staying put (60%) so the player can catch him.

THIEF STEALS. When the thief and the player are in the same room, small chance per turn he lifts a treasure from the player's inventory. Per the plan, no engine 'co-located' Condition exists; we OR over (playerAt(R) AND itemAt(thief, R)) for each wanderable room. Each branch only fires if the player carries that treasure. Treasures pulled from the win-condition list. Stealing flips the item's location to 'thief' (he holds them).

branches.narration: A black-gloved hand snakes toward your pocket — you slap it away.
'A trifle, surely?' the thief murmurs, and the egg vanishes from your possession.
'How droll.' The bag of coins slips from your grasp into the thief's cloak.
The chalice is plucked from your hand with a flick of the thief's wrist.
The painting — gone. The thief inclines his head in mock apology.
The jade figurine disappears into the folds of the thief's cloak.
The scarab — gone in a heartbeat. The thief tips an imaginary hat.
'A pleasure.' The bracelet slides off your wrist into the thief's palm.

triggers.$comment: THIEF BLADED CLASS TRIGGER. Player-initiated combat with a bladed weapon. Thief is fast and slippery — high miss rate, modest damage when you hit, instant-kill on a critical. Priority 10.

branches.narration: Your blade scores a shallow cut across the thief's arm. He hisses.
Your blade bites deep. The thief reels, blood staining his fine cloak.
A clean killing strike. The thief sinks to his knees — 'How... unfortunate' — and topples.
The thief sidesteps your blade with a graceful pivot.

triggers.$comment: THIEF DIES. Drops everything he was carrying back to the room (including stolen treasures). The thief item itself goes 'nowhere'.
triggers.narration: The thief's body crumples. His cloak falls open and a glittering hoard of treasures spills out across the floor. Wherever he stashed them, they have all returned to the Treasure Room — that, perhaps, was his real lair.

triggers.$comment: PUZZLE #2 COFFIN AT ALTAR — canonical: SOUTH-TEMPLE-FCN sets COFFIN-CURE = NOT(coffin in inventory) every turn at south-temple. We mirror with two symmetric triggers — one to set the flag when player arrives without coffin, one to clear it if they re-pick up the coffin. The down→tiny-cave exit unblocks/re-blocks dynamically.
triggers.narration: A cool draft seems to flow up from the floor of the temple — a passage somewhere has opened.
As you take the coffin in hand, the draft from below ceases. The way down has sealed itself again.

triggers.$comment: PUZZLE #6 ECHO ROOM — canonical: bare 'echo' verb in loud-room's special command loop sets LOUD-FLAG and clears SACREDBIT on the platinum bar. We use visibleWhen on the bar (already added in items overrides) so the bar materializes once echo-spoken is true. Removes the matched intent so it can fire on later visits if needed (though it's gated on echo-spoken=false so it's effectively once).
triggers.narration: Your voice goes out — and instead of the cacophony you've been suffering, perfect silence answers. The acoustics of the room have changed. A platinum bar gleams on the floor where the noise had hidden it.

triggers.$comment: PUZZLE #3 WAVE SCEPTRE AT RAINBOW — canonical: SCEPTRE-FUNCTION sets RAINBOW-FLAG and clears INVISIBLE on POT-OF-GOLD. We use visibleWhen on pot-of-gold (already added in items overrides). Skip the canonical toggle-off-on-second-wave; once solid, stays solid.
triggers.narration: You raise the sceptre and trace a slow figure in the air. The rainbow shudders and solidifies — its colors hardening into a luminous bridge of gold and prismatic light. At your feet, a heavy pot of gold appears, gleaming where the rainbow now touches earth.

triggers.$comment: PUZZLE #4 ENDGAME — when the win condition is met, set won-flag (which unblocks west-of-house→stone-barrow). Narrates the canonical 'almost inaudible voice' line. once:true. Kept as a regular trigger so it fires on the same turn the win condition becomes true (cascading through the fixed-point loop).
triggers.narration: An almost inaudible voice whispers in your ear: 'Look to your treasures for the final secret.' Across the room, the trophy case glitters; in the depths of the wood, an ancient map appears — and far above ground, the way to a stone barrow stands open.

triggers.$comment: Stone-barrow finale — canonical STONE-BARROW-FCN runs FINISH (game over with credits). We end the game when the player enters.

effects.message:
**** You have completed the Great Underground Empire ****

A voice calls out from the depths of the barrow: 'Welcome, adventurer. Your final score: {flag.score} (out of {flag.max-score}), in {flag.global-turn-count} moves. This earns you the rank of {rank}. Your name shall be inscribed in the chronicles of those who have braved the Empire and prevailed.'

The stone slab settles back into place behind you. Your adventure is at an end.

triggers.$comment: PUZZLE #5 MIRROR ROOM — RUB the mirror to teleport between mirror-room-1 and mirror-room-2. (Canonical also swaps room contents to maintain the 'same room' illusion; we skip that — the LLM can narrate the disorientation.)
triggers.narration: You touch the cool surface of the mirror — and feel a brief, dizzying lurch. The room around you seems unchanged, yet subtly different, as if you've stepped through a hidden door.
You touch the cool surface of the mirror — and feel a brief, dizzying lurch. The room around you seems unchanged, yet subtly different, as if you've stepped through a hidden door.

triggers.$comment: PUZZLE #8 BAT CARRY — when the player is in bat-room without garlic, the vampire bat seizes them and drops them at a random underground location (canonical FLY-ME picks from BAT-DROPS). Gated on bat-carried-this-visit so the trigger fires once per visit, not every fixed-point iteration. Reset trigger below clears the flag when player leaves the room.

branches.narration: Fweep! The bat seizes you by the scruff of the neck and lifts you away through utter blackness. You are deposited, dazed, in a coal mine.
Fweep! The bat carries you through tunnels you cannot see, depositing you in another part of the mine.
Fweep! The bat hauls you off into the darkness and drops you somewhere deep underground.
Fweep! The bat seizes you and bears you off to a distant shaft.
Fweep! The bat carries you up a narrow shaft and drops you at the top.
Fweep! The bat plummets with you down a long, dark drop and releases you at the bottom.
Fweep! The bat takes you on a winding journey and drops you in a squeaky-floored cavern.
Fweep! The bat carries you back near where the mining began.

triggers.$comment: Reset bat-carried-this-visit when player is no longer in bat-room, so the bat will grab them again on the next visit (canonical Zork lets the bat re-grab on every entry).

PUZZLE #1 BELL + BOOK + CANDLES — adapted from canonical (which has strict bell→candles→book sequence with bell becoming hot, candles dropping, etc.). Our simplified take: ring the bell anywhere (sets bell-rung); candles start lit per extraction; reading the book at entrance-to-hades while bell-rung AND candles-lit triggers lld-flag.
triggers.narration: The bell tolls once — a deep, hollow note that hangs in the air far longer than seems natural. The metal grows warm in your grasp.

triggers.$comment: Capstone of the LLD ritual — read the book at entrance-to-hades while bell-rung and candles still lit. Sets lld-flag, opening the in/south exits to land-of-living-dead.
triggers.narration: You read the words from page 569 aloud — and they hang in the air like smoke. The shrieks of the unseen wraiths fall silent, the candles flicker as if in a sudden wind, and a passage gapes open in the wall ahead. The Land of the Dead lies beyond.

triggers.$comment: PUZZLE #7 COAL → DIAMOND MACHINE — canonical: TURN switch WITH screwdriver only fires the conversion if machine lid is CLOSED. We gate the intent on machine being closed; this trigger additionally checks coal is inside the machine. On success: coal → nowhere, diamond → machine-room (player can then take it).
triggers.narration: The machine comes to life with a dazzling display of colored lights and bizarre noises — clattering, whirring, sparks. After a few moments the racket subsides. The lid pops open. Where you placed the coal, a magnificent diamond now lies.

triggers.$comment: Failure case: TURN switch with non-coal in machine (or empty). Canonical produces 'gunk'. We just narrate frustration to keep the puzzle solvable without polluting the inventory.
triggers.narration: The machine clatters and whirs, but produces only a faint puff of greenish smoke. Whatever it's looking for, it isn't finding.

triggers.$comment: PUZZLE #9 DAM CONTROL PANEL — multi-step. YELLOW button enables bolt control (gate-flag). BROWN button disables it. With gate-flag set, TURN BOLT WITH WRENCH at dam-room toggles gates-open. Opening gates queues an 8-turn delay (low-tide-countdown). When countdown hits 0, low-tide flag flips → reservoir exits open.
triggers.narration: Click. A green bubble on the panel flickers and glows steadily.
Click. The green bubble dims and goes dark.

triggers.$comment: Bolt toggles the gates open (when currently closed). Starts the 8-turn drain countdown.
triggers.narration: The bolt turns slowly. Somewhere below, the sluice gates open and water pours through the dam with a sustained roar. The reservoir will take some time to drain.

triggers.$comment: Bolt toggles the gates closed (when currently open). Cancels the drain.
triggers.narration: The bolt turns the other way. Below, the sluice gates rumble shut, and water begins to back up behind the dam once again.

triggers.$comment: Tick the drain countdown each turn while gates are open and countdown > 0. Mirrors the lamp battery / grue tick patterns.
When countdown hits 0 and gates still open, set low-tide. Once-only narration (the reservoir cascades will re-flood if gates close, but we don't re-narrate every cycle).
triggers.narration: Far below, the reservoir empties at last. What was deep water is now mud and silt — passable, if unpleasant.

triggers.$comment: PUZZLE #10 (bonus) EMPTY-HANDED COAL MINE — canonical: timber-room ↔ lower-shaft is a narrow squeeze that requires the player to carry NOTHING. Tracks inventoryCount in real time via two symmetric triggers; flag flips automatically on take/drop. The flag-gated exits (timber-room.west and lower-shaft's two return exits) open/close as a side effect. Player must drop the lit lamp before squeezing through; the lamp glows on the floor of the room they leave.
BOAT INFLATE — pump turns the deflated boat into a seaworthy vehicle. The vehicle.enterableWhen on the item gates board()ing on this state automatically.
triggers.narration: The boat hisses and swells into a sturdy yellow raft, ready for the river.

triggers.$comment: BOAT REPAIR — putty closes the puncture. Returns boat to deflated state (must be re-inflated). Putty consumed.
triggers.narration: You smear the putty over the gash. With a final hiss the rip seals; the boat is patched, deflated, and ready to inflate again. The tube of putty is now empty.

triggers.$comment: WEAPON-IN-VEHICLE PUNCTURES BOAT — the canonical 'thin plastic vs sharp object' check, done in the simplest possible framing: player is in the boat AND has any item tagged weapon → boat punctures, player ejected back to dam-base. Catches both the boarding-with-weapon case AND any other path that leaves a weapon in the boat-with-player state. Tag-driven so any new weapon (just give it tag:weapon) automatically participates.
triggers.narration: Something sharp slips — the boat hisses, sputters, and collapses to a useless pile of plastic. You're spat out onto the riverbank at the foot of the dam.

triggers.$comment: RIVER CURRENT TICKS — increment a counter each turn the player is in-boat at any river-N. The advance triggers (next block) gate on the counter reaching 2 (gives player one full turn at each river to choose where to land before the current carries them). Five separate triggers per river so afterAction:once-each prevents cascading all the way down in one turn.

RIVER CURRENT ADVANCE — when the per-river counter hits 2, advance player + boat one room downstream and reset counter. River-5 doesn't have an advance — it has the waterfall-death trigger instead. Threshold of 2 means player gets the arrival turn + 1 more turn to land before drifting on.
triggers.narration: The current carries you downstream.
The current carries you downstream, around a bend.
The current carries you faster — the rumble ahead grows louder.
The current sweeps you forward; the roar of falling water fills your ears. This is your last chance to land — east to the sandy beach!

triggers.$comment: RIVER-5 WATERFALL DEATH — at river-5 with counter >= 2, player goes over Aragain Falls. Threshold same as the others (2 ticks of grace). Player has one full turn at river-5 to escape east → shore.

effects.message:
**** You have died ****

Unfortunately, the magic boat doesn't provide any protection from the rocks and boulders one meets at the bottom of waterfalls. Including this one. Aragain Falls roars its indifference.

$winConditionsComment: Win when ALL canonical Zork I treasures (incl. those previously unwired) are deposited in the trophy case. Three new treasures from the Phase-4 puzzle batch: bar (echo-room), pot-of-gold (rainbow), diamond (coal-machine).

winConditions.message: An almost inaudible voice whispers in your ear, 'Look to your treasures for the final secret.' The trophy case glitters; the Great Underground Empire is conquered.
